title: Address Before the Young Men's Lyceum of Springfield, Illinois
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.1 pp.108-115
inclusion_rationale: Earliest major speech. Proto-covenant and proto-organism metaphors before Lincoln has political stakes — valuable baseline.
known_limitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

title: Temperance Address
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.1 pp.271-279
inclusion_rationale: Body/disease cluster in alcohol reform context — establishes pre-political use of organic metaphors.
known_limitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

title: Eulogy on Henry Clay
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.2 pp.121-132
inclusion_rationale: Ancestral-debt cluster. Lincoln positions Clay as exemplar of covenant-keeping.
known_limitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

title: Speech at Peoria, Illinois
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.2 pp.247-283
inclusion_rationale: First major deployment of experiment/proposition frame re: slavery. 17,000 words.
known_limitations: Reported or extemporaneous text may contain transcription noise; exact wording and span boundaries require caution.

title: House Divided Speech
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.2 pp.461-469
inclusion_rationale: Body-organism cluster at full intensity. Dual activation: house = body AND household covenant.
known_limitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

title: First Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Ottawa, Illinois
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.3 pp.1-37
inclusion_rationale: Metaphors under adversarial pressure. Annotate Lincoln turns only.
known_limitations: Debate text depends on nineteenth-century newspaper transcriptions; exact wording and span boundaries require caution. Annotation and analysis are limited to Lincoln turns.

title: Second Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Freeport, Illinois
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.3 pp.38-76
inclusion_rationale: Proposition cluster under maximum argumentative pressure.
known_limitations: Debate text depends on nineteenth-century newspaper transcriptions; exact wording and span boundaries require caution. Annotation and analysis are limited to Lincoln turns.

title: Third Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Jonesboro, Illinois
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.3 pp.102-144
inclusion_rationale: Southern IL audience — watch for metaphor suppression vs. northern venues.
known_limitations: Debate text depends on nineteenth-century newspaper transcriptions; exact wording and span boundaries require caution. Annotation and analysis are limited to Lincoln turns.

title: Fourth Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Charleston, Illinois
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.3 pp.145-201
inclusion_rationale: CRITICAL for absence analysis: Lincoln suppresses equality-proposition cluster here. Tag explicitly.
known_limitations: Debate text depends on nineteenth-century newspaper transcriptions; exact wording and span boundaries require caution. Annotation and analysis are limited to Lincoln turns.

title: Fifth Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Galesburg, Illinois
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.3 pp.207-244
inclusion_rationale: Compare cluster activation with Jonesboro (suppressed) and Galesburg (receptive).
known_limitations: Debate text depends on nineteenth-century newspaper transcriptions; exact wording and span boundaries require caution. Annotation and analysis are limited to Lincoln turns.

title: Sixth Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Quincy, Illinois
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.3 pp.245-325
inclusion_rationale: Included as the sixth Lincoln-Douglas debate for full debate-series coverage and register-controlled comparison.
known_limitations: Debate text depends on nineteenth-century newspaper transcriptions; exact wording and span boundaries require caution. Annotation and analysis are limited to Lincoln turns.

title: Seventh Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Alton, Illinois
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.3 pp.283-325
inclusion_rationale: Final debate — Lincoln's closing synthesis after 7 rounds of adversarial pressure.
known_limitations: Debate text depends on nineteenth-century newspaper transcriptions; exact wording and span boundaries require caution. Annotation and analysis are limited to Lincoln turns.

title: Address at Cooper Institute, New York City
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.3 pp.522-550
inclusion_rationale: Best single document for fathers/inheritance cluster. Lincoln cites founders by name.
known_limitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

title: Farewell Address at Springfield, Illinois
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.4 pp.190-191
inclusion_rationale: Providence cluster in unguarded form. Lincoln speaking extemporaneously about his own death.
known_limitations: Two textual versions circulate; variant-sensitive claims should identify the version used.

title: First Inaugural Address
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.4 pp.262-271
inclusion_rationale: Retain as a Lincoln-primary inaugural address while applying sentence-level Seward controls: received text, Lincoln-adopted wording, and strict Lincoln-origin evidence.
known_limitations: Includes Seward-origin/revised closing passages; authorship-sensitive analysis should caveat inst_00076 and exclude inst_00077 from Lincoln-sole or strict Lincoln-origin claims.

title: Message to Congress in Special Session, July 4, 1861
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.4 pp.421-441
inclusion_rationale: Experiment/proposition most explicit here. Note how metaphors operate in administrative register.
known_limitations: Administrative register constrains metaphor density and should be controlled before comparing to formal addresses.

title: Fragment on the Constitution and the Union
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.4 pp.168-169
inclusion_rationale: Lincoln privately reasons Union is 'picture of silver', liberty the 'apple of gold'. Rare explicit metaphor theorizing. High epistemic value.
known_limitations: Private fragment with uncertain date; diachronic placement should be treated cautiously.; Private fragment has limited audience context and short length; frequency claims require caution.

title: Letter to Horace Greeley
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.5 pp.388-389
inclusion_rationale: CRITICAL. 'If I could save the Union without freeing any slave I would do it.' Metaphor suppression event. Written 2 months before Preliminary Emancipation. Track covenant-over-freedom suppression.
known_limitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

title: Preliminary Emancipation Proclamation
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.5 pp.433-436
inclusion_rationale: Deliberately flat. Absence of birth/body clusters is the finding. Use as control document.
known_limitations: Legal register intentionally suppresses metaphor; absence findings should be interpreted as register-controlled evidence, not as source failure.

title: Annual Message to Congress, December 1, 1862
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.5 pp.518-537
inclusion_rationale: 'Last best hope of earth' — experiment frame at high intensity in administrative register.
known_limitations: Administrative register constrains metaphor density and should be controlled before comparing to formal addresses.

title: Emancipation Proclamation
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.6 pp.28-30
inclusion_rationale: Compare to preliminary — same flatness. The document actually freeing enslaved people contains almost no metaphor. Flatness is data.
known_limitations: Legal register intentionally suppresses metaphor; absence findings should be interpreted as register-controlled evidence, not as source failure.

title: Letter to James C. Conkling
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.6 pp.406-410
inclusion_rationale: Written to be read aloud at rally. Defends emancipation to war Democrats. Track obligatory frame distribution across racial lines.
known_limitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

title: Address Delivered at the Dedication of the Cemetery at Gettysburg
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.7 pp.22-23
inclusion_rationale: 272 words. Birth, proposition, sacrifice, ancestral-debt all present. Highest metaphor density. Use Bliss copy. Structurally a birth narrative — 'brought forth' in line 1 reprised in 'new birth of freedom'. Sacrificial economy fully operational.
known_limitations: Multiple manuscript witnesses exist; variant-sensitive claims should identify the copy used.

title: Memorandum Concerning His Probable Failure of Re-election
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.7 pp.514-515
inclusion_rationale: Private note sealed from cabinet. Providence cluster in purely private form. Key test: does public providence rhetoric reflect genuine private cognition?
known_limitations: Private fragment has limited audience context and short length; frequency claims require caution.

title: Letter to Albert G. Hodges
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.7 pp.281-283
inclusion_rationale: 'I claim not to have controlled events but confess plainly that events have controlled me.' Obligatory frame and lincoln_non_agent as Lincoln's explicit self-description. Canonical. Also proto-Second-Inaugural theodicy.
known_limitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

title: Response to a Serenade, November 10, 1864
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.8 pp.96-97
inclusion_rationale: Extemporaneous. Experiment/proof frame unguarded — election result as empirical evidence.
known_limitations: Reported or extemporaneous text may contain transcription noise; exact wording and span boundaries require caution.

title: Second Inaugural Address
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.8 pp.332-333
inclusion_rationale: All clusters present. Providence/theodicy at full development. Distributed guilt. 'The Almighty has His own purposes.' Obligatory frame at maximum. Wound-and-healing closes. Diachronic endpoint.
known_limitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

title: Last Public Address
source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.8 pp.399-405
inclusion_rationale: Reconstruction policy. Wound-and-healing now programmatic. Diachronic endpoint for organism cluster. Delivered 4 days before assassination.
known_limitations: No special provenance caution recorded.
